data.title: Vertiv: Strong Q1 Beat, Margin Expansion, and Capacity Investments Underpin Buy Rating and Upgraded 2026 Outlook
data.description:
William Blair analyst Brian Drab has maintained their bullish stance on VRT stock, giving a Buy rating yesterday.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Brian Drab has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Vertiv’s very strong first-quarter performance with robust organic revenue growth that slightly exceeded expectations and a sharp improvement in profitability, as reflected in substantial operating margin expansion and EPS meaningfully above consensus. He notes that these results were supported by higher volumes, better productivity, and effective pricing actions that more than compensated for headwinds such as tariffs.

In addition, Drab points out that Vertiv is committing capital to expand capacity in both products and services, positioning the company to capture future demand and sustain growth. Even though Vertiv has stopped disclosing backlog and orders each quarter, management indicates that the order pipeline remains healthy and is projected to increase into 2026, and this confidence is reinforced by the company’s issuance of second-quarter guidance alongside an upward revision to its 2026 outlook.

In another report released yesterday, TD Cowen also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $347.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Calix: Transition to Calix One and Stable Long-Term Outlook Underpin Buy Rating Despite Near-Term Headwinds
data.description:
Calix, the Technology sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst today. Analyst Ryan Koontz from Needham assigned a Buy rating on the stock and has a $62.00 price target.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Ryan Koontz has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to Calix’s recent performance and strategic transition. The company posted first-quarter 2026 results at the top end of guidance and issued second-quarter expectations roughly in line with the Street, even as higher memory component prices temporarily weigh on gross margins but are being pushed through to customers.

He also views the near-term pause in software bookings as transitory, driven by Calix and its clients concentrating on migrating from an AWS-based cloud stack to the new GCP-powered agentic Calix One platform. Following the launch of the Calix 3.0 vision, a bolder operating model, and stable earnings projections through fiscal 2028 despite a modest price-target trim to $62, he sees a path back to robust double-digit software growth and supports a Buy stance on the shares.

Koontz covers the Technology sector, focusing on stocks such as Adtran, Calix, and Lantronix. According to TipRanks, Koontz has an average return of 37.2% and a 53.20% success rate on recommended stocks.

In another report released yesterday, J.P. Morgan also maintained a Buy rating on the stock with a $65.00 price target.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk

data.title: Hold Rating Maintained as Semiconductor Order Rebound Clashes with Modest Revenue Momentum
data.description:
Analyst David Farrell from Jefferies maintained a Hold rating on XP Power and keeping the price target at p1,140.00.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

David Farrell has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors including a sharp rebound in first‑quarter orders, particularly from semiconductor customers, which signals that key end markets are emerging from a period of inventory reduction and should support revenue growth. However, management itself cautions that such elevated order levels are unlikely to be sustained, implying that the recent strength may reflect restocking dynamics rather than a fully established upcycle.

At the same time, reported revenue growth remains modest, with quarter‑on‑quarter sales declining in line with seasonal patterns and the impact of lost export licences and production shifts, limiting near‑term earnings momentum. Consensus expectations for the full year therefore remain unchanged, suggesting limited scope for positive estimate revisions or multiple expansion in the short term, and justifying a neutral, Hold stance pending clearer evidence of a durable growth acceleration.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
